title: Waiver for citizenship requirements  - Canada.ca
content:
## Waiver for citizenship requirements
To become a Canadian citizen, you must meet certain requirements when you apply. If you can’t meet a specific requirement, you can request an exemption. This is called a waiver. If your waiver is approved, it means you don’t need to meet that requirement.
You can only request a waiver for
- the citizenship test
- proving your English or French language skills
- the oath of citizenship
If you can meet the requirement but you need help with it , request an accommodation instead .
Examples of an accommodation are:
- other accessible versions of the test, such as Braille, large-print, or oral tests
- extra time to take the test for someone with a disability
- sign language interpretation and wheelchair access at citizenship events (like a ceremony or an interview)
Learn more about accommodations (opens in a new tab)

## For more information
Learn about all the requirements to become a Canadian citizen .

### Give feedback about this page
Thank you for your feedback.
